mechanism_narrative: Rising real retail sales (RSAFS) signal strong consumer spending, which drives corporate earnings growth and pushes S&P 500 prices higher over the subsequent month. This positive relationship is persistent across the business cycle because consumption is the largest component of GDP.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Spearman rank correlation between RSAFS monthly changes and S&P 500 20-day forward returns is negative or below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate drops below 0.50%, monetary policy is extremely accommodative. This compresses discount rates, encourages leverage, and boosts equity valuations. Historically, such episodes generate positive short-term S&P 500 returns as markets price in persistent easy money.
failure_condition: Average 5-day forward return during triggered months is not significantly greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05) or is negative.

mechanism_narrative: When both employment (PAYEMS) and retail sales (RSAFS) are above their respective 6-month moving averages, it confirms broad-based consumer strength. The joint signal reduces noise from single-series fluctuations and reliably predicts positive S&P 500 returns as the economy expands.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered non-overlapping windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects strengthening manufacturing activity. In the early-cycle recovery regime (first 12 months after a recession trough), this expansion is not yet inflationary and directly translates into higher equity prices as earnings expectations improve.
regime_hint: early-cycle recovery (first 12 months after recession trough)
failure_condition: INDPRO increases do not predict positive 60-day forward returns in identified early-cycle periods; rank IC < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) erodes the real value of future corporate earnings and forces the central bank to tighten policy. This increases discount rates and compresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month regardless of the starting level.
failure_condition: Rolling 5-year Spearman correlation between CPIAUCSL monthly changes and S&P 500 20-day forward returns is positive or above -0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A falling unemployment rate below 5.0% signals a tight labor market where wage pressures compress corporate margins and lead to pre-emptive Fed tightening. This environment historically depresses equity prices as cost pressures accumulate and valuations contract.
failure_condition: Average 20-day forward return during triggered months is positive, or less than 55% of triggered periods produce negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 1.0% and industrial production is above its neutral level (100), it indicates that monetary accommodation is actively supporting real economic growth. This combination is particularly bullish as it suggests sustained expansion without immediate tightening risk.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields positive 60-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances over any 5-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) are a leading indicator of residential investment and consumer confidence. During a post-recession housing recovery (with starts below 1.5 million units and trending upward), rising HOUST reliably forecasts expanding economic activity and higher equity prices over the next quarter.
regime_hint: post-recession housing recovery (HOUST < 1.5M and trending up from trough)
failure_condition: In the identified recovery regimes, the correlation between HOUST changes and 60-day forward returns is negative or rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year period.
